MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2017 Advanced From Feb-14-17 To Feb-09-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Weiss (415-554-3824) Deputy City Attorney for Plaintiff/opposing party City and County of San Francisco.
Matt Metzger (415-513-5980) of Belvedere Legal for Defendant/Moving party JOEL ELLIOTT.

The Court announced the tentative ruling. Plaintiff submits to tentative ruling.
ARGUED. Defendant argues 473.(D). Plaintiff responds. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOEL ELLIOTT MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party fails to sustain burden under CCP 473(b), 473(d), 473.5 or equitable grounds. Order is signed in open court. (see scanned order for details). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-28-2016 Off Calendar

Motion For Default Judgment And Permanent Injunction Against Defendant Joel Elliott
Off Calendar Sep-29-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing. Dept. 514, Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: A. Mok. Proceedings reported by: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Michael S. Weiss, Esq., appearing for Plaintiffs, City and County of San Francisco, A
Municipal Corporation, and The People of the State of California,
Order Granting Waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing for Default Prove-up on file.
Defendant Joel Elliot present in court,
Default of Defendant has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant as follows:

Injunction and Judgment of $1,696,272.63 for Plaintiffs against Defendant ELLIOTT represents the following civil penalties, attorneys' fees and costs:

1. Pursuant to San Francisco Housing Code section 204(c)(2), $930,000 in civil penalties for violations of the San Francisco Housing Code at the Property from January 2, 2014 through July 22, 2016 (930 days @ $1,000 per day) shall be recovered by the City and County of San Francisco.

2. Pursuant to San Francisco Building Code section 103A, $574,000 in civil penalties for violations of the San Francisco Building Code at the Property from May 30, 2013 through July 22, 2016 (1,148 days @ $500 per day) shall be recovered by the City and County of San Francisco.

3. Pursuant to Business and Professions Code section 17206, $137,500 in civil penalties for violations of Business and Professions Code sections 17200-17209 at the Property between
November 2011 to July 2016 (55 months @ $2500/month) shall be recovered by the State of California.

4. Pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 11581(b)(2), $25,000 in civil penalties for violations of the Drug Abatement Act at the Property shall be recovered by the State of California.

5. Pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 17980.7(d)(1) and Civil Code section 3496(c), PLAINTIFFS' reasonable attorneys' fees in the amount of $29,670 shall be recovered by the City and county of San Francisco.

6. Pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 17980.7(d)(1), PLAINTIFFS' reasonable costs in the amount of $102.63 shall be recovered by the City and county of San Francisco.

As for the $1,696,272.63 in civil penalties, attorney's fees and costs, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO shall recover $1,533,772.63 and PLAINTIFF PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA shall recover $162,500.00.

IT FURTHER ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that PLAINTIFFS shall be entitled to recover all
reasonable attorneys' fees and costs associated with enforcing this Default Judgment and
Injunction, including all fees and costs incurred to collect this Judgment.

The Court expressly reserves jurisdiction to take such further action as may be necessary or
appropriate to carry into effect the provisions of this Default Judgment and Injunction.

Judgment signed in open court.
